hops.context: with Loopy’s cells (green) rotating opposite to its morphology (blue and yellow).
the morphology’s rotation in the environment is the sum of its rotation relative to its cells and the cell’s rotation in the environment (11).
while ωmc measures the rate at which the lobed morphology shifts along the cellular chain.

question: In the context of Loopy's cellular and morphological interactions, how is the angular velocity ωmc of the lobed morphology in relation to its cellular environment determined?

options.A: It is calculated by summing the opposite rotational angular velocity of the cells (ωce) to the morphology’s angular velocity (ωme).
options.B: It is determined by dividing the morphology’s angular velocity (ωme) by the rotational angular velocity of the cells (ωce).
options.C: It is derived by multiplying the rotational angular velocity of the cells (ωce) with the morphology’s angular velocity (ωme).
options.D: It is found by subtracting the rotational angular velocity of the cells (ωce) from the morphology’s angular velocity (ωme).

explanation: According to the paper, ωmc, the angular velocity of the morphology in its environment, is the sum of the morphology’s angular velocity (ωme) and the opposite rotational angular velocity of the cells (ωce). Thus, ωmc is calculated by summing these two velocities, where the cells' rotation is considered in the opposite direction to the morphology’s rotation.
